# NewFile

> NewFile is a free, open-source macOS Finder Sync Extension that adds a "New Text File" option to the Finder right-click menu and toolbar. macOS has never shipped this functionality natively; NewFile fills the gap with a small, native, signed, notarized binary that runs as a Finder extension.

## What it does

- Adds **New Text File** to the Finder right-click menu in folders where the extension is enabled.
- Adds a **NewFile** toolbar button (drag-to-customize from `View → Customize Toolbar…`).
- Creates `New Text File.txt` (or `New Text File 2.txt`, `New Text File 3.txt`, …) in the active folder.
- Reveals and selects the newly created file so you can rename it immediately.

## Who it's for

- macOS users who came from Windows or Linux and miss the right-click → New File workflow.
- Developers and writers who frequently create scratch / config / prompt files.
- Anyone tired of opening TextEdit just to make an empty `.txt`.

## Install

- Homebrew: `brew install --cask newfile`
- Manual: download `NewFile.dmg` from <https://github.com/mariusgm/newfile/releases/latest>

## System requirements

- macOS 13 (Ventura) or later. Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).
- On macOS Sequoia 15.0–15.1, the System Settings Extensions UI is buggy; update to 15.2 or later if the toggle doesn't appear.

## Privacy

NewFile collects nothing, sends nothing, contacts no servers, and has no analytics. The website (newfile.dev) is the same — no cookies, no trackers, no third-party fonts.

## Licensing and source

- License: MIT
- Source code: <https://github.com/mariusgm/newfile>
- Releases: <https://github.com/mariusgm/newfile/releases>
- Latest version: v0.1.0 (signed with Developer ID, notarized by Apple, stapled)

## Contact

The project is maintained by Marius Garberg Mevold. Issues and PRs welcome at the GitHub repository above.
